oatmeal-apple-cookies-lezzet.name: Oatmeal Apple Cookies
instructions: 1. Blend flour, oatmeal, carbonate, vanilla and salt in a bowl.
2. As for the residue of butter and sugar mixer in a large container, gang.
3.   Add only one to the ovuls.
4. Mix suffice and milk add and mix thoroughly.
5. Finally add cube apples.
6. parish with spoon from the spoon;as well as intermittent to the oiled paper-line tray.
7. Cook for 12-14 minutes in oven with a set of 180 degrees.
8. Cool the grill wire in the veuuml;
9. Imari usulu fill the made of melt and paper.
10. Create lines and serve after freezing in the kitchen.

ocopa-step-by-step-comedera.name: Ocopa, Step By Step

ingredients.item: 150 gr de queso fresco o serrano

instructions: Let's start our recipe, warming up a frying pan with olive oil.
Cut the onion, yellow and yellowish interiors into julians or thin strips. Trocks garlic and huacatay.
When the oil's hot, we're gonna have to drink the garlic, then the onion. Later, the Aids and finally you will add the Huacatay. It's cold for five minutes. Kill and let rest.
In a blender or processor, add evaporate milk. Then you'll have to add some frying vegetables and get out.
Then we'll add minced cheese and cookies that will give you texture. Loan till you have a homogenous and well-processed sauce.
Cut the potatoes in half, and you'll scatter his Ocopa sauce all over his surface.
Now, you'll be ready to eat. Drop it!

octopus-pasta.name: Octopus Pasta
instructions: Cook 12 oz. linguine in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until al dente. Drain, reserving 1 cup pasta cooking liquid.

Meanwhile, heat 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add ½ cup panko, season with kosher salt, and cook, stirring often, until golden brown and crisp, about 3 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl. Wipe out skillet.

Heat 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in same skillet over medium-high. Add two 4.2-oz. cans octopus, drained, patted dry, and cook, tossing occasionally, until golden brown and crisp, 2–3 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a plate.

Add remaining 1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil to same skillet. Cook 5 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped, and 3 Tbsp. coarsely chopped drained capers, stirring often, until slightly softened, about 2 minutes. Add ¼ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes and a big pinch of salt; season generously with freshly ground black pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, until combined and fragrant, about 1 minute. Add 1 cup dry white wine and cook, stirring often, until reduced by two thirds, about 4 minutes.

Add pasta, ½ cup (packed) finely chopped parsley, 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, and reserved ½ cup pasta cooking liquid to skillet; reduce heat to medium-low. Cook, tossing often and adding remaining pasta cooking liquid a little at a time as needed, until pasta is coated. Remove from heat. Add octopus, then finely grate zest from 1 lemon over. Cut lemon in half. Squeeze in juice from 1 half and toss to combine. Cut remaining half into wedges.
Divide pasta among shallow bowls and top with panko mixture. Serve with lemon wedges for squeezing over.

old-fashioned-pumpkin-butter.name: Old Fashioned Pumpkin Butter
instructions: Combine pumpkin puree, sugar, maple syrup, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, cloves, salt, cayenne pepper, apple juice, and apple cider vinegar in a deep, heavy-bottomed saucepan; whisk until thoroughly combined. Place over medium-high heat and stir occasionally until simmering.

Reduce heat to medium-low and cook, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and reduces by about half, 45 to 60 minutes. The sauce will be very hot, so wear an oven mitt and stir carefully. You may also want to use a splatter guard to keep the sauce from dirtying your stove.
Once reduced, remove from the stove and let cool for 10 minutes. Toss in butter and whisk until it disappears. Let cool to room temperature, about 1 1/2 hours.
Transfer pumpkin butter into an airtight container and place in the refrigerator until thoroughly chilled and flavors have developed, 8 hours to overnight. Store in the refrigerator.

olive-brined-air-fryer-turkey-breast.name: Olive-brined Air Fryer Turkey Breast

ingredients.item: Brine from a can of olives

instructions: Whisk together olive brine and buttermilk. Put the turkey breast into a resealable plastic bag and pour brine-buttermilk mixture into the bag. Add rosemary and thyme sprigs. Seal bag and refrigerate for 8 hours.
Take the bag out of the fridge and allow to rest until the breast reaches room temperature.
Preheat an air fryer to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Cook the breast in the air fryer for 15 minutes. Flip over the breast and cook for 5 minutes until turkey breast is no longer pink in the center and the juices run clear. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 74°C (74 degrees C). If internal temperature is lower, keep cooking at 5 minute intervals until the correct temperature is reached.

omelet-recipe-with-spinach-very-comedera.name: Omelet Recipe With Spinach, Very
omelet-recipe-with-spinach-very-comedera.original_name: Receta de tortilla con espinacas, muy fácil
instructions: We'll start with very good washing of spinach and eliminating any ground residue or other dirt. I'd rather put them in a big paddle and put them under a cold jet of water, to help me with water pressure as well. If you also wish to disinfect them, you can soak them in a bowl with water mixed with a vinegar jet for at least five minutes.

Then we'll scold the spinach and get them into pieces. We will soon warm up water in a saucer and once we get to boiling, we will add the spinach with a touch of salt and cook them for fifteen minutes.
Mientras tanto, aprovecharemos este tiempo para pelar y picar muy finamente la cebolla y el ajo.
When the spinach's cooked, we'll scold them. Then we will warm an oil jet into a frying pan and we will drink the onion and garlic we have minced recently.
We'll let both ingredients sigh for five minutes and then we'll put in the padded spinach for five more minutes.

While we cook the vegetables, we'll caress the eggs into a bowl. Then we will put out the frying pan fire, and we will add the spinach to the egg mix, to revere everything vigorously after adding a touch of salt and another of pepper to taste.

We will rinse the frying pan used previously and once again heat an oil chorrit with medium-low heat. Once hot, we will pour the blend of eggs and vegetables (care that they are well distributed throughout the frying pan and cook the omelette till it cuts. To validate that it has been curved at the bottom, we can slightly move the frying pan with the handle. If the omelette isn't attached to the bottom and it's baked, we'll feel a slight shift and we'll know that it's the right time to turn it around.

Turn the omelette with another frying pan or plate. Let it be cooked from the other end but note that the richest omelets are the ones we don't let get completely into the centre. The cooking should accordingly be ready for a few minutes.
Retira la tortilla del calor y deja que repose algunos minutos. Eso es todo, ya tienes tu plato listo para servirse.

one-pan-mashed-potatoes.name: One-pan Mashed Potatoes

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 3 0.5 teaspoons morton kosher salt

instructions: Real Talk: Are you in a huge hurry to get everything on the table? If you want to save yourself an extra 10 minutes, you don’t have to peel the potatoes. Also, you’ll get the best, fluffiest, smoothest consistency using a ricer—but it’s definitely not required! Go ahead and mash away your sorrows with whatever you’ve got on hand.

Cook shallots, garlic, and schmaltz in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until very soft and golden, 15–20 minutes. Add potatoes, cream, milk, thyme sprigs, and salt and bring to a simmer, skimming occasionally, until potatoes are fork-tender, 20–25 minutes. Reserve ¾ cup of that wonderfully starchy cooking liquid; set aside.

Using a ricer, potato masher, or fork and some inner rage, smash potatoes directly in pot. Add cooking liquid to thin, give your potatoes a taste, and season with salt if needed. Transfer to a platter, then top with thyme leaves.
Do Ahead: Mashed potatoes can be made 3 hours ahead; reserve extra cooking liquid. Cover and store at room temperature. Reheat over low, adding warmed cooking liquid to thin if necessary.

one-pan-tartiflette.name: One-pan Tartiflette

ingredients.item: Yukon gold or other waxy potatoes

ingredients.prep: or use chicken or vegetable stock

ingredients.item: Soft cow’s-milk cheese with bloomy rind
. sherry vinegar or apple cider vinegar
Pinch of freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.prep: such as endive, radicchio, frisée, or arugula

instructions: Make the tartiflette: Heat the oven to 400ºF.
Add the bacon to a cold large ovenproof skillet and place the pan over medium heat. Let the bacon cook, stirring occasionally, until the fat renders and the edges crisp slightly, 7 to 10 minutes.

Add the onions to the skillet and raise the heat to medium high. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions are golden and soft, 7 to 10 minutes. Stir in the garlic, thyme, and nutmeg, and cook for another minute, until fragrant.

Add the potatoes, wine, salt, and pepper and toss until well combined. Cover the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes are just tender and the wine has mostly evaporated, 20 to 25 minutes. If the pan dries out while the potatoes are cooking, add a splash of water.

Slice the cheese ¼-inch thick (you can leave the rind on). Stir the crème fraîche into the potatoes, making sure they are evenly coated. Lay the cheese slices in an even layer on top of the potatoes. Bake, uncovered, until the cheese melts and the potatoes are very tender, 25 to 35 minutes. Serve hot or warm.

While the tartiflette is in the oven, make the salad: In a small bowl, whisk together the vinegar, salt, and pepper. Whisk in the oil. In a salad bowl, toss together the lettuces, pear, and parsley. Toss with enough of the dressing to coat everything. Taste and add more salt and vinegar, if needed. Serve alongside the tartiflette.

one-pot-beer-cheese-bucatini.name: One-pot Beer Cheese Bucatini

ingredients.prep: smoked cheddar, or american cheese, coarsely grated (about 1 cup)

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or 1 0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt
-oz. bottles porter or other dark beer

instructions: Toss together 8 oz. sharp cheddar, coarsely grated (about 2 cups), and 4 oz. Gruyère, smoked cheddar, or American cheese, coarsely grated (about 1 cup), in a large bowl with your hands. Sprinkle 1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. cornstarch over and toss until cheese is evenly coated. Set cheese mixture aside.
Place 4 cups salted pretzels in a large resealable plastic bag; seal and crush with a rolling pin until the texture of coarse breadcrumbs (some larger pieces are okay).

Melt 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot (make sure your pot is wide enough to fit your pasta lengthwise). Add pretzel crumbs and cook, stirring constantly, until browned and crisp, 6–8 minutes. Transfer to a bowl; wipe pot and reserve. Add ⅓ cup finely chopped parsley to pretzel crumbs and season with kosher salt and freshly ground pepper. Set seasoned pretzel crumbs aside.

Bring two 12-oz. bottles porter or other dark beer (about 3 cups) and 2½ cups water to a boil in reserved pot. Add 1 lb. bucatini and cook, stirring very gently with a heatproof rubber spatula to keep pasta from breaking, until about half of liquid is absorbed and pasta is very al dente, 7–9 minutes (about 3 minutes less than package directions).

Add 1 cup whole milk, 3 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce, 2 Tbsp. spicy brown mustard, 1 Tbsp. onion powder, 2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt, ¼ tsp. cayenne pepper, and remaining 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter to pasta and return to a boil. Cook, stirring often, until pasta is al dente, about 2 minutes (taste a strand to check). Remove from heat and add reserved cheese mixture a handful at a time, stirring with spatula after each addition until mostly melted before adding more. If sauce is too thick, stir in warm water ½ cup at a time until desired consistency is achieved. Taste pasta and season with more salt and pepper if needed.
Divide pasta among shallow bowls and top with reserved seasoned pretzel crumbs.

one-pot-fennel-and-sausage-pasta.name: One-pot Fennel And Sausage Pasta
instructions: Add the sausage to a large pot on medium high. Cook until browned. Remove temporarily from the pot.
Add sliced fennel, onion, garlic, oregano, salt and pepper. Cook until the onions and fennel are golden and caramelized.
Add the tomato paste, chopped tomatoes, and meat. Stir and cook until the tomato paste has darkened and is aromatic.
Bring to a boil, reduce to simmer, and cook until the sauce thickens slightly.
Add the cooked pasta, the grated parmesan, and the mozzarella pearls. Stir until well combined and heated through.
Garnish with fennel fronds, (the green leaves of the fennel bulb).
Enjoy!

one-skillet-lemony-chicken-with-fennel-a.name: One-skillet Lemony Chicken With Fennel And Tomatoes
instructions: Place a rack in top third of oven; preheat to 425º. Pat 6 chicken thighs dry with paper towels; season all over with salt.

Place a large ovenproof skillet on stovetop and pour in 2 Tbsp. oil. Transfer chicken thighs to skillet, skin side down, and heat over medium. (That's right: Those chicken thighs are going into a cold pan, and then you turn the heat on. Trust!) Cook, rotating skillet to encourage even browning, until opaque around the edges and skin is deeply golden brown, 8–11 minutes. Transfer skin side up to a plate.

Meanwhile, do some prep: First, remove fennel fronds from 1 fennel bulb and reserve for making the sauce. Cut fennel head in half lengthwise. Place halves cut side down on cutting board, then cut each half into ½" wedges.
Peel and slice 2 shallots into quarters lengthwise.
Cut 1 tomato crosswise into ½" rounds.
Trims ends off 1 lemon. Cut half of the lemon into thin rounds (about ⅛"). Reserve remaining half of lemon for later.

Adjust heat to medium-high and add sliced fennel, shallots, and lemon rounds to skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until fennel just begins to soften, about 4 minutes. Season with salt and pepper and give mixture another stir. Remove skillet from heat.
Arrange tomato rounds and chicken over fennel mixture; season with salt and pepper.

Transfer skillet to oven and roast until chicken thighs are cooked through (an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part near the bone should register 165°), juices run clear, and vegetables begin to caramelize around edges of pan, 16–20 minutes. Carefully remove skillet from oven and let rest 5 minutes.
While chicken is roasting, finely chop reserved fennel fronds until you have about ¼ cup.
Squeeze juice from reserved lemon half into a small bowl. Add chopped fronds to bowl and stir in remaining ¼ cup oil; season with salt and pepper.
Spoon fennel oil over chicken and serve with toast alongside.

onion-tuna-teleculinaria.name: Onion Tuna
instructions: Tempere o atum com sal e pimenta e core-o, de ambos os lados, numa frigideira com o azeite bem quente, durante cerca de 3 a 5 minutos. Retire e reserve.
Peel the potatoes, cut them in half and bake them in salt water. Peel the onions and cut into thin half-moons. Peel the garlic and lamine them.
Na mesma frigideira, e aproveitando o azeite do atum, refogue a cebola e o alho com o louro. Regue com o vinho branco e deixe ferver. Adicione a manteiga e deixe engrossar.
Junte novamente o atum e as batatas e envolva. Polvilhe com ervas frescas picadas a gosto e sirva.
Tempere o atum com sal e pimenta e core-o, de ambos os lados, numa frigideira com o azeite bem quente, durante cerca de 3 a 5 minutos. Retire e reserve.
Peel the potatoes, cut them in half and bake them in salt water. Peel the onions and cut into thin half-moons. Peel the garlic and lamine them.
Na mesma frigideira, e aproveitando o azeite do atum, refogue a cebola e o alho com o louro. Regue com o vinho branco e deixe ferver. Adicione a manteiga e deixe engrossar.
Junte novamente o atum e as batatas e envolva. Polvilhe com ervas frescas picadas a gosto e sirva.

orange-marmalade.name: Orange Marmalade

ingredients.item: Seville oranges or other sour oranges (2 lb./1 kg total weight)

instructions: Squeeze the juice and seeds from 6 Seville or other sour oranges, halved and 1 navel orange, preferably organic, halved into a mesh strainer set over a small bowl. After all the oranges have been squeezed, place seeds on a piece of cheesecloth, gather up the sides, and tie securely. Cut each rind in half and then slice as thinly as possible.

Put the orange slices in a large pot or a Dutch oven. Add the reserved orange juice and bag of seeds, a pinch of kosher or canning salt, and 10 cups water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then decrease the heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes. Remove from the heat, cover, and let stand overnight at room temperature.

The next day, stir in 8 cups (1.6 kg) sugar and set the pot over high heat. Bring the mixture to a boil, then decrease the heat to medium-low to maintain a gentle simmer. Cook, stirring occasionally to make sure the mixture is not burning on the bottom, until the marmalade reaches the jelling point (104°C on an instant-read or candy thermometer; or use the wrinkle test to judge when it’s done by spooning a drop of the marmalade onto a small plate that’s been chilled in the freezer and pushing it with your finger—if the marmalade wrinkles, it’s ready, if it’s still syrupy, keep boiling). If white scum occasionally rises to the top, skim it off with a large spoon.

Stir in 2 Tbsp. (30 ml) cognac or whiskey (if using) and, using a pair of tongs, remove the bag of seeds, squeezing with the tongs to extract as much marmalade from it as possible. Ladle the hot marmalade into clean jars. Wipe rims clean with a damp cloth, cover tightly, let cool, and refrigerate.
Do Ahead: Marmalade can be made 6 months in advance and stored in the refrigerator. After opening, consume within 1 month.
Editor’s note: This marmalade recipe was first printed online in December 2011.

orange-peel-sweet-spoon-argiro.name: Orange Peel Sweet Spoon
instructions: We choose oranges large fat plums without blows with tight skin and flesh.
Wash the oranges and dry them.
With very fine scrubbing, gently remove the orange zest surface.
With a sharp knife remove horizontally a small slice from both sides of the fruit, the top and bottom.
Squeeze along in four and remove the peels with great care to prevent them breaking. In the end, we'll have 32 orange peels.
Wrap each orange peel in a tight roll, fasten with a toothpick or alternatively pass a thick thread with a thick needle and make an orange peel necklace.
Add a large pot with water to boil.
In boiling water that continues to boil, dip the orange peels for 4-5 minutes to boil.
Remove the orange peels and place them in a colander.
Change the water and repeat the same process to boil the orange peels for 2-3 minutes this time.
We repeat once again.
After boiling the orange peels for the third time, remove them from the hot water and place them in a large bowl with cold water.
Leave the orange peels for 12 hours in the cold water. In between this time we change the water 3-4 times.
After 12 hours we remove the peels from the water and put them on a clean absorbent towel.
Remove the supports and tampon well for the towel to draw as much water as possible.
Now orange peels have taken the shape of a roll and will keep it throughout the boiling.
Place a pot with a thick base on the heat (we need a large pot wide).
Add the sugar to the pot with the water and mix.
Allow the syrup to boil and boil for 5 to 6 minutes.
Then, add the orange carrot peels inside.
Simmer for 30 minutes the sweet orange peel and place it in the pot until it cools completely.
After the spoon is completely cold, we need to put it back on fire to reheat and tie the syrup properly for a few minutes.
Add the lemon juice and allow it to boil for 1-2 minutes.
The sweet orange peel must cool in the pot. Without lid, leave the pot covered simply with a towel, until the sweet spoon and syrup cool completely.
We have ready sterile jars and lids and we equalise the cold now sweet to the sterile jars.

originals-oven-roasted-chicken-salad-mas.name: Originals Oven Roasted Chicken Salad Mason Jar

ingredients.item: Dietz & watson originals oven roasted chicken breast

instructions: In small bowl, combine Originals Oven Roasted Chicken Breast, Greek yogurt, red onion, celery and lemon juice.
Divide chicken mixture, spring mix, cranberries, apples and pecans in half.
To assemble, begin by placing chicken and dressing mixture into the bottom of 2 jars.
Next add lettuce then finish by topping with cranberries and pecans and tighten lids.
Once ready to eat, empty jar into a bowl, mix and enjoy!

oven-baked-fish-recipe-teleculinaria.name: Oven Baked Fish Recipe
oven-baked-fish-recipe-teleculinaria.original_name: Receita de peixe assado no forno

ingredients.item: 4 medium fish or 2 large to taste

instructions: Dawn the fish and season them with wine, tomato pulp, chopped garlic, salt, pepper, lemon juice, laurel, chopped coriander and thyme leaves.

Cut an onion into slices and spread it on a tray. Have the fish on top and then the tomato, previously cleaned from seeds and cut into cubes. Water everything with half the olive oil and put it in the oven, preheated at 185°C, for about 30 to 40 minutes.
Descasque e corte a restante cebola em gomos. Limpe os pimentos e corte-os em tiras. Descasque a cenoura e corte-a em fatias finas. Lave a curgete e corte-a da mesma forma.

Light a frying pan with the rest of the olive oil and, when it is hot, add the onions and carrot slices and saute them. Add the peppers and, shortly thereafter, the curget. Season with salt, pepper and other thyme leaves, stir and let cook, but so that the vegetables do not get too cooked. Remove the fish from the oven and serve it with the vegetables.

oven-roasted-beets-with-wilted-spinach.name: Oven-roasted Beets With Wilted Spinach
instructions: Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C).
Trim and wash beets. Set leaves aside; discard stems. Wrap beets in 2 aluminum foil packages (3 beets per foil package); place in a deep baking dish.
Roast in the preheated oven until tender, about 45 minutes.
Divide onions, herbes de Provence, 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper between packages. Reseal packages; roast 45 minutes more.
Divide and drizzle balsamic vinegar between packages. Reseal packages; roast 5 minutes more.

Meanwhile, melt butter with remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in a sauté pan over medium heat. Add garlic; cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add spinach and reserved beet greens; cook and stir until wilted, about 5 minutes.
Transfer beets, onions, and drippings to a serving bowl; stir in wilted greens. Season with salt and black pepper.

oven-roasted-delicata-squash.name: Oven-roasted Delicata Squash

ingredients.prep: seeded, and cut into thin slices

instructions: Preheat oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Combine sliced delicata squash and olive oil in a large bowl and mix well to cover squash with oil. Season with salt. Spread out in 1 layer on a baking sheet.

Bake in the preheated oven until squash is soft and lightly browned, turning after 10 minutes, about 25 minutes total. Remove baking sheet from oven, season with salt, maple syrup, and curry powder and bake an additional 3 minutes.
